revision.comment: Bot: mark 1 deleted QID(s) as DELETED_QID in ill templates [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24167314344|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:千鹿頭神]] {{infobox deity|Japanese Deity Infobox |ja| name = Chikatō Kami |ja| generation = {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|en|lt=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} 3rd Generation |ja| predecessor = {{ill|Mote Takami|1=Moritaku-no-Kami|2=ja|3=守宅神|lt=Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神|1=Moritaku-no-Kami|2=ja|3=守宅神|lt=Moritaku-no-Kami|en|lt=Mote Takami|1=Moritaku-no-Kami|2=ja|3=守宅神|lt=Moritaku-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mote Takami|1=Moritaku-no-Kami|2=ja|3=守宅神|lt=Moritaku-no-Kami|qq=}} |ja| successor = {{ill|Kodama Hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|en|lt=Kodama Hiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodama Hiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |ja| type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} |ja| Full_name = Chikatō Kami |ja| alias = Chikata no Kami, Chikata, Chikato, and others |ja| alternative_name = {{ill|Uchi Prefecture Kami|ja|内県神|en|lt=Uchi Prefecture Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchi Prefecture Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135551174|1=Uchi-agata-no-Kami}} |ja| Divine_Rank = |ja| aspect = God of Hunting, Mountain Deity |ja| Mausoleum = |ja| father = {{ill|Mote Takami|1=Moritaku-no-Kami|2=ja|3=守宅神|lt=Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神|1=Moritaku-no-Kami|2=ja|3=守宅神|lt=Moritaku-no-Kami|en|lt=Mote Takami|1=Moritaku-no-Kami|2=ja|3=守宅神|lt=Moritaku-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mote Takami|1=Moritaku-no-Kami|2=ja|3=守宅神|lt=Moritaku-no-Kami|qq=}} (according to 『Shuya Clan Genealogy』)
{{ill|Moreya|en|Moreya|qid=Q11146526|lt=Moriya|1=Moreya}} (theory)
{{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391|lt=Take Minakata|1=Takeminakata}} (theory) |ja| mother = |ja| spouse = {{ill|Uragokohime-no-Mikoto|ja|宇良古比売命|en|lt=Uragokohime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uragokohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Chikatō Kami''' (千鹿頭神, Chikatō Kami or Chikatou-no-Kami) is a {{ill|Shinto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} deity ({{ill|Folk religion|en|Folk religion|qid=Q1068640|lt=folk belief|1=Folk religion}}) primarily worshiped in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, as well as in {{ill|Eastern Japan|ja|東日本|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Japan|qq=}} regions such as {{ill|Koshin region|ja|甲信地方|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshin region|1=Kōshin region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3209390}}, {{ill|North Kanto|ja|北関東|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North Kanto|1=Kita Kantō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5366730}}, and {{ill|Southeast Tohoku|ja|南東北地方|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southeast Tohoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. [[File:Aruga Chikatō Shrine - 有賀千鹿頭神社.jpg|thumb|Chikatō Shrine located in the former {{ill|Toyoda Village, Suwa District|ja|Toyoda Village|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoda Village, Suwa District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|en|Suwa, Nagano|qid=Q846338|lt=Suwa City|1=Suwa, Nagano}} Toyoda Aruga)]] == Overview == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|345x345px|Family tree of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} according to 『Shuya Clan Genealogy』 showing the first five generations (from Moriya to Hachikushi Kami)]] In the folk traditions of the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} ({{ill|Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa faith|ja|諏訪信仰|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社}}), Chikatō Kami is considered the offspring deity of {{ill|Moreya|en|Moreya|qid=Q11146526|lt=Moriya|1=Moreya}} and his descendants, or alternatively, an alias of that deity. Chikatō Kami is sometimes equated with {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata's|1=Takeminakata}} offspring deity {{nihongo|Uchi Prefecture Kami|内県神|en|lt=Uchi Prefecture Kami|qq=}}. According to the 『Shuya Clan Genealogy』, Chikatō Kami is the son of {{ill|Mote Takami|1=Moritaku-no-Kami|2=ja|3=守宅神|lt=Moritaku-no-Kami|ja|洩宅神|1=Moritaku-no-Kami|2=ja|3=守宅神|lt=Moritaku-no-Kami|en|lt=Mote Takami|1=Moritaku-no-Kami|2=ja|3=守宅神|lt=Moritaku-no-Kami|1=Moritaku-no-Kami|lt=Moritaku-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mote Takami|1=Moritaku-no-Kami|2=ja|3=守宅神|lt=Moritaku-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritaku-no-Kami|qq=}}, the son of {{ill|Moreya|en|Moreya|qid=Q11146526|lt=Moriya-no-Kami|1=Moreya}}, and is counted as the third generation of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|en|lt=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}. His name originates from an event where his father, while hunting, captured 1,000 deer, hence the name "Chikatō" (千鹿頭, meaning "thousand deer head").
Chikatō Kami was born with extraordinary spiritual powers, taking over hunting duties from his father, and followed the Great Deity in hunting and game. He obtained a thousand deer. He had one son, whom he named Chikatō Kami. Chikatō Kami succeeded his father in overseeing ceremonial affairs.
Ancient {{ill|Kagura|ja|kagura|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagura|qq=}} songs: :Chikatō no Kitano Hayashinowa Susumushiwa Susumushiwa Yachiyo no Koyete Tsunenitaisenu :Chikatō no Myōjin Shiyushūreshito Ohosoransu Ohosoransu Yukitataima no Hana no Kiyomeyo Chikatō's shrines are located in {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|en|Suwa District, Nagano|qid=Q1131473|lt=Suwa District|1=Suwa District, Nagano}} in Aruga, Uehara, Hanahara, Yokobuki, Kyudo, {{ill|Higashichikuma District, Nagano|en|Higashichikuma District, Nagano|qid=Q1155105|lt=Higashichikuma District|1=Higashichikuma District, Nagano}} Kanda, Hayashi both located in {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|Iida|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida, Nagano|1=Iida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11224660}} District, {{ill|Fukushima District, Fukushima|ja|Fukushima|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima District, Fukushima|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Prefecture, and in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|qid=Q132720|lt=Toyama Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}} and {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}} among others. In the area {{ill|Urakoyama|ja|宇良古山|en|lt=Urakoyama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urakoyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is situated, there exists a shrine dedicated to him. {{ill|Kodama Hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|en|lt=Kodama Hiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodama Hiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, the offspring of the Great Deity's child {{ill|Kataura Hade-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命|en|lt=Kataura Hade-no-Mikoto|1=Katakurabe no Mikoto|lt=Katakurabe no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kataura Hade-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakurabe no Mikoto|qq=}}, is considered the successor of Chikatō Kami. He married {{ill|Mitasama Hime-no-Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神|en|lt=Mitasama Hime-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitasama Hime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the child of {{ill|Moritachi no Kami|lt=Moritachi no Kami|lt=Moritatsu-no-Kami|ja|守達神|en|lt=Moritachi no Kami|lt=Moritatsu-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritatsu-no-Kami|qq=}}, and gave birth to {{ill|Hachikushi|ja|八櫛神|en|lt=Hachikushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachikushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Suwa Education Association. "『Shuya Clan Genealogy』" 『Suwa Historical Documents Collection, Volume 28』, 1938, pp. 31-72.Nomoto Mikato. "Approach to Chikatō Kami" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, pp. 333-336.Imai Nohiku. "O Suwa-sama Faith and Kami no Naga" 『Gods' Village: Ancient Suwa Stories』, Kokusho Publishing, 1976, pp. dd7-dd8. According to the 『Genealogy』, Chikatō Kami married {{ill|Uragokohime-no-Mikoto|ja|宇良古比売命|en|lt=Uragokohime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uragokohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and moved to {{ill|Urakoyama|ja|宇良古山|en|lt=Urakoyama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urakoyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now {{ill|Matsumoto, Nagano|en|Matsumoto, Nagano|qid=Q213324|lt=Matsumoto City|1=Matsumoto, Nagano}}, {{ill|Kanda, Matsumoto|ja|神田|en|lt=Kanda|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanda, Matsumoto|1=Shinden (divine field)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10855080}}), which suggests that Chikatō Kami was either exiled from Suwa or left voluntarily. It is also believed that the succession of Chikatō Kami by {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391|lt=Take Minakata|1=Takeminakata}}'s grandchild {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|en|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}} indicates that the {{ill|Suwa clan|en|Suwa clan|qid=Q2975376|lt=Suwa Clan|1=Suwa clan}} (descendants of the mythological {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391|lt=Take Minakata|1=Takeminakata}}) took over the local worship duties of indigenous forces. There are also traditions where the foreign-born Chikatō Kami married local daughters not only in Matsumoto but also in other places. A famous tale narrates that "Chikato Kami" from Shinshu came to {{ill|Akagiyama Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|en|lt=Akagiyama Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagiyama Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}} and married the sister of the {{ill|Akagi Shrines|ja|赤城神|en|lt=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Imai Nohiku. "Chikato-sama" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, pp. 325.Nomoto Mikato. "Approach to Chikatō Kami" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, pp. 342. == Faith == According to Imai Nohiku's research on the distribution of {{ill|Mishagujishin|ja|ミシャグジ信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishagujishin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Chikatō Kami shrines are found not only in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} (13 shrines) but also in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|qid=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}} (8 shrines), {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}} (12 shrines), {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}} (20 shrines), {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}} (12 shrines), {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|qid=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}} (7 shrines), and {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|qid=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}} (15 shrines), spreading from Kanto to Tohoku. They have various representations such as "Chikatō," "Chikata," "Chikato," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," and "Chikata," with pronunciations including "Chikata," "Chikatsu," "Chikato (u)," etc.Imai Nohiku. "Chikatō Kami Approach" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, pp. 326.Nomoto Mikato. "Approach to Chikatō Kami" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, pp. 343. The distribution of Chikatō Kami shrines is characterized by their apparent movement along mountainous regions (such as the {{ill|Yatsugatake|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugatake|qq=}}, {{ill|Haruna|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haruna|qq=}}, {{ill|Akagi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi|qq=}}, {{ill|Mount Ontake|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ontake|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamigomezaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamigomezaki|qq=}}) and is believed to have spread through the eastward expansion of hunting clans (descendants of the {{ill|Moriya|ja|Moriya clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). As deities of hunting, they are closely related to {{ill|Mishagujishin|ja|ミシャグジ信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishagujishin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Tenpaku faith|lt=Tenpaku faith|lt=Tenpaku Faith|ja|天白信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Faith|qq=}}. From Imai's research, it is evident that in Yamanashi, Saitama, and Gunma, {{ill|Mishagujishin|ja|ミシャグジ信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishagujishin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} shrines coincide with Chikatō Kami shrines.Yamato Iwao. "Shinano Ancient History Study" 『Shinano Ancient History Study』, Meicho Publishing, 1990, pp. 201-202.Imai Nohiku. "Survey Collection of Shrine Priests" 『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2017, pp. 212-281. In addition to hunting deities from Suwa (Shinshu), there are regions where Chikatō Kami is also revered as a deity of agriculture and fire prevention, similar to {{ill|Mishagujishin|ja|ミシャグジ信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishagujishin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Tenpaku faith|lt=Tenpaku faith|lt=Tenpaku Faith|ja|天白信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Faith|qq=}}, with many shrines worshiping them as {{ill|Sekibō|en|Sekibō|qid=Q17994957|lt=Sekibō|1=Sekibō}} deities.Nomoto Mikato. "Approach to Chikatō Kami" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, pp. 347-349.Imai Nohiku. "Survey of Shrine Priests" 『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2017, pp. 300-301.Imai Nohiku. "Survey of Shrine Priests" 『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2017, pp. 338-339.Tanaka Moto. "Primitive Agricultural Ritual Elements in Mote Yashima Ceremonial System" 『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2017, p. 192.. Moreover, many shrines have different main deities depending on the region, even within {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. There are still many unexplored shrine precincts and regions, leaving numerous mysteries unresolved. === Main Shrines === ==== Koshin Region ==== * {{ill|Kanda Chikatō Shrine|ja|神田千鹿頭神社|en|lt=Kanda Chikatō Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanda Chikatō Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hayashi Chikatō Shrine|ja|林千鹿頭神社|en|lt=Hayashi Chikatō Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Chikatō Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, {{ill|Matsumoto, Nagano|en|Matsumoto, Nagano|qid=Q213324|lt=Matsumoto City|1=Matsumoto, Nagano}} - Hataiwa) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社 (長野県諏訪市豊田有賀)|en|lt=Chikatō Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135551175|1=Chikato Shrine}} ({{ill|Suwa, Nagano|en|Suwa, Nagano|qid=Q846338|lt=Suwa City|1=Suwa, Nagano}}, Toyoda Aruga) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社 (長野県茅野市ちの上原)|en|lt=Chikatō Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135551176|1=Chikato Shrine}} ({{ill|Chino, Nagano|en|Chino, Nagano|qid=Q838660|lt=Chino City|1=Chino, Nagano}}, Chino Uehara) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社 (長野県茅野市米沢埴原田)|en|lt=Chikatō Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135551178|1=Chikato Shrine}} ({{ill|Chino, Nagano|en|Chino, Nagano|qid=Q838660|lt=Chino City|1=Chino, Nagano}}, Yoneda Hanahara) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社 (長野県諏訪郡富士見町横吹)|en|lt=Chikatō Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135551179|1=Chikato Shrine}} ({{ill|Yokobuki, Suwa City|ja|横吹|en|lt=Yokobuki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokobuki, Suwa City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Suwa City) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社 (長野県上伊那郡辰野町樋口)|en|lt=Chikatō Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135551180|1=Chikato Shrine}} ({{ill|Kamiina District, Nagano|en|Kamiina District, Nagano|qid=Q1155054|lt=Higashiina District, Ichigata Town|1=Kamiina District, Nagano}}, Higashiina District, Ichigata Town) * {{ill|Chikatsu Shrines|ja|近津神社|en|lt=Chikatsu Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikata Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|qid=Q201932|lt=Kofu City|1=Kōfu}}, Kiyatecho) * {{ill|Chikatsu Shrines|ja|近戸神社|en|lt=Chikatsu Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikata Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|qid=Q201932|lt=Kofu City|1=Kōfu}}, Takamachi) ==== Kanto Region ==== * {{ill|Oogo Shrine|ja|大胡神社|en|lt=Oogo Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oogo Shrine|qq=}} (Former Chikata Shrine) ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|qid=Q201613|lt=Maebashi City|1=Maebashi}}, Kawaharaha) * {{ill|Chikatsu Shrines|ja|近戸神社|en|lt=Chikatsu Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikata Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kasukawa Town|ja|粕川町|en|lt=Kasukawa Town|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukawa Town|1=Kasukawamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q27501298}}) * {{ill|Chikatsu Shrines|ja|近戸神社|en|lt=Chikatsu Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikata Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Battoi Town|ja|笂井町|en|lt=Battoi Town|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battoi Town|1=Utsuboimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28682222}}) * {{ill|Oyato Shrine|ja|親都神社|en|lt=Oyato Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyato Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135551181|1=Oyato Shrine}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Agatsuma District, Gunma|en|Agatsuma District, Gunma|qid=Q642394|lt=Agatsuma District, Nakanojō Town|1=Agatsuma District, Gunma}}) * {{ill|Chihō Shrine (Kazo Central)|ja|千方神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chihō Shrine (Kazo Central)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135550938|1=Chihō Shrine (Kazo Central)}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama
